title: AQL Sampling Risk & Cost Calculator
description: Calculates the total cost of quality risk and sampling inspection based on Acceptable Quality Level (AQL), lot size, inspection level, and defect cost parameters. Aligns with ISO 2859-1, Six Sigma, and Lean principles.

inputs.label: Lot Size (N)
inputs.businessContext: Total number of items in the inspection lot per ISO 2859-1.

businessContext_i18n.en: Total number of items in the inspection lot per ISO 2859-1.

inputs.label: AQL (%)
inputs.businessContext: Acceptable Quality Level – maximum percent defective that is considered acceptable as a process average.

businessContext_i18n.en: Acceptable Quality Level – maximum percent defective that is considered acceptable as a process average.

inputs.label: Inspection Level
inputs.businessContext: General or special inspection level per ISO 2859-1. Level II is normal.

businessContext_i18n.en: General or special inspection level per ISO 2859-1. Level II is normal.

inputs.label: Sampling Plan Type
inputs.businessContext: Switching rules for severity of sampling per ISO 2859-1.

businessContext_i18n.en: Switching rules for severity of sampling per ISO 2859-1.

inputs.label: Unit Cost
inputs.businessContext: Cost per unit item (production or purchase cost).

businessContext_i18n.en: Cost per unit item (production or purchase cost).

inputs.label: Defect Cost per Unit
inputs.businessContext: Cost incurred if a defective unit reaches the customer (rework, warranty, loss of goodwill).

businessContext_i18n.en: Cost incurred if a defective unit reaches the customer (rework, warranty, loss of goodwill).

inputs.label: Inspection Cost per Unit
inputs.businessContext: Cost to inspect one unit (labor, equipment, overhead).

businessContext_i18n.en: Cost to inspect one unit (labor, equipment, overhead).

inputs.label: Actual Defect Rate (p)
inputs.businessContext: Estimated true defect rate in the lot (from historical data or process capability).

businessContext_i18n.en: Estimated true defect rate in the lot (from historical data or process capability).

inputs.label: Confidence Level
inputs.businessContext: Statistical confidence level for risk calculations.

businessContext_i18n.en: Statistical confidence level for risk calculations.

rules.message: Lot size must be at least 2 and AQL between 0.01% and 10%.
Actual defect rate must be between 0% and 100%.
Valid inspection level and sampling plan type required.

thresholds.message: Actual defect rate exceeds 1.5x AQL – high risk of rejecting good lots or accepting bad lots.
Inspection cost per unit exceeds 50% of unit cost – consider reducing inspection or using alternative methods.
Sample size exceeds 20% of lot – consider switching to reduced inspection if justified.

formulas.label: Sample Size (n)
formulas.description: Determined from ISO 2859-1 master table based on lot size, inspection level, and sampling plan type.

formulas.label: Accept Number (Ac)
formulas.description: Maximum number of defectives allowed in the sample for lot acceptance.

formulas.label: Probability of Acceptance (Pa)
formulas.description: Probability that the lot will be accepted given actual defect rate, using binomial or Poisson distribution.

formulas.label: Producer's Risk (α)
formulas.description: Probability of rejecting a lot with defect rate equal to AQL (Type I error).

formulas.label: Consumer's Risk (β)
formulas.description: Probability of accepting a lot with defect rate equal to LTPD (typically 2x AQL).
formulas.equation: β = Pa(p = LTPD) where LTPD = 2 * AQL

formulas.label: Expected Defect Cost
formulas.description: Expected cost from defects that pass through inspection (accepted lots) plus cost of rejected lots (scrap/rework).
formulas.equation: ExpectedDefectCost = (1 - Pa) * lot_size * defect_rate_actual * defect_cost_per_unit + Pa * lot_size * defect_rate_actual * defect_cost_per_unit * (1 - inspection_effectiveness)

formulas.label: Total Risk Cost (Primary Result)
formulas.description: Sum of inspection cost, expected defect cost, and sampling risk cost (producer + consumer).
formulas.equation: TotalRiskCost = (sample_size * inspection_cost_per_unit) + ExpectedDefectCost + (producer_risk * lot_size * unit_cost * 0.1) + (consumer_risk * lot_size * defect_cost_per_unit * 0.2)

primary.label: Total Risk Cost
primary.description: Total expected cost combining inspection, defect, and sampling risks.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: High Actual Defect Rate
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Actual defect rate significantly above AQL increases consumer risk and defect cost.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Low Inspection Effectiveness
hiddenLossDrivers.description: If inspection does not catch all defects, hidden cost increases.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Sampling Plan Mismatch
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Using normal plan when tightened is needed increases risk.

suggestedActions.label: Reduce Actual Defect Rate
suggestedActions.description: Implement process improvement (Six Sigma DMAIC) to lower p below AQL.

suggestedActions.label: Switch to Tightened Inspection
suggestedActions.description: If defect rate is consistently above AQL, use tightened plan per ISO 2859-1 switching rules.

suggestedActions.label: Increase Sample Size
suggestedActions.description: Use higher inspection level (e.g., III) to reduce consumer risk.

suggestedActions.label: Automate Inspection
suggestedActions.description: Reduce inspection cost per unit via automated vision or sensor systems.

dataConfidenceAdjusted.label: Data Confidence Adjusted Total Risk Cost
dataConfidenceAdjusted.description: Total risk cost adjusted by a confidence factor based on sample size and historical data reliability.
